meaning: vi.变动,波动,涨落,上下,动摇vt.使动摇,使波动,使起伏
examples: Body temperature can fluctuate if you are ill.
The analysis reveals that intensity appears to fluctuate with a clear, well-defined rhythm.
Later on in the early years of the nineteenth century, when Fort Vancouver became the headquarters of the Hudson's Bay Company, deer populations continued to fluctuate.
In a mark-to-market system, the value of loans would fluctuate according to changes in interest rates.
The wholesale energy market is volatile, and prices for electricity per kilowatt-hour can fluctuate daily.
